bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. All issues have valueC as the top utility (1.00), so I'll bid valueC for everything. My highest weight issue is issueF (0.3), followed by issueC, issueD, issueE (0.2 each), and issueA, issueB (0.1 each). Starting strong with all valueC gives me full utility of 1.0 and sets an anchor for the negotiation.

The opponent opened with all valueA (their best, my worst), giving me utility of 0.2 across all issues = 0.2 total. We're very early (t=0.02), so I should stay near my maximum. I'll hold firm at my top bid (all valueC = utility 1.0) since there's no reason to concede yet. The opponent's opening was their ideal, so this is just the standard opening exchange.

We're very early (t=0.04) and both sides are at their extreme positions. The opponent is offering all valueA (which gives me 0.20 utility on each issue, total ~0.20), while I've been offering all valueC (utility 1.0 for me). Since it's still very early, I should stay close to my ideal but make a small concession to signal willingness to negotiate. I'll concede on the lowest-weight issues (issueA and issueB, weight 0.1 each) by moving to valueB, while holding firm on the higher-weight issues.

The opponent has been consistently offering all valueA except they just conceded issueD to valueC in their latest offer. This signals issueD might be less important to them, and they're willing to give it up. My highest weight issue is issueF (0.3), so I should hold firm there. The opponent seems to care most about getting valueA on most issues. I'll concede on low-weight issues (issueA, issueB) to valueA while holding firm on high-weight issues (issueF, issueE, issueC, issueD). This gives me utility: 0.1*0.2 + 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.3*1.0 = 0.02 + 0.02 + 0.2 + 0.2 + 0.2 + 0.3 = 0.94, which is still very high while showing some concession on low-weight issues.

The opponent has been consistently offering all valueA (their best), but I can see some movement: at t=0.06 they gave issueD: valueC, and now at t=0.08 they've moved to issueB: valueB, issueD: valueB, issueF: valueB. This suggests they're starting to concede on lower-priority issues for me. My highest weight issue is issueF (0.3), and the opponent just offered valueB there - that's a signal. I should hold firm on issueF (valueC) and issueE (valueC) since they're my top priorities, but I can concede on lower-weight issues (issueA, issueB) to valueA to show movement. We're still early (t=0.08) so I don't need to rush, but I should show some flexibility.

The opponent has been consistently offering all valueA (their best), but their latest offer at t=0.10 shows some movement: issueA: valueB, issueD: valueB, issueE: valueB, issueF: valueB - they're conceding on the issues that matter most to me (issueF, issueD, issueE). This is a positive signal. My current utility from their latest offer: 0.1*0.61 + 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.2*0.61 + 0.2*0.61 + 0.3*0.61 ≈ 0.061+0.02+0.04+0.122+0.122+0.183 = 0.548.
